component_identification.content:
#### Board & Tiles
- **1 game board** — Hex-grid map of the Kingdom of Pomme with Castle Everfree at the center, 8 outer territories, and surrounding base camps.
- **8 base camp tiles** — One per animal kingdom, placed around the board's outer edge.
- **4 power-up tiles per player** — 2 Move power-ups and 2 Make power-ups (32 total). Each enhances one action when unlocked.

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- **16 seeker miniatures** — 2 seekers per animal kingdom (foxes, bears, bunnies, owls, turtles, mice, pandas, and cats). Each pair shares a color.
- **8 trophy tokens** — Flat tokens in each player color used to mark earned trophies on the trophy track.
- **24 pie tokens** — Used for combat bidding and quest completion. Stored behind player screen.
- **8 friendship tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Friendship Track printed on the board.
- **1 trophy track** — Printed on the board with 8 trophy categories.

#### Resources
- **20 apple tokens** — Red resource tokens placed on the map via Seek actions.
- **20 gem tokens** — Blue resource tokens placed on the map via Seek actions.

#### Cards & Dice
- **24 quest cards** — Draw when landing on a quest space; present a choice with friendship consequences.
- **16 magic spell cards** — Crafted via Make action; provide one-time special abilities.
- **4 story dice** — Custom dice showing apple, gem, and quest symbols used for the Seek action.
- **8 player screens** — Hide pie tokens and spell cards from opponents.

#### Solo Mode
- **1 Automountie board** — AI opponent reference card for solo play.
- **Automountie tokens** — Movement and decision tokens for the solo opponent.

#### Miscellaneous
- **1 first-player token** — Adorable pie-shaped marker.
- **1 rulebook** — Includes standard rules, solo rules, and variant suggestions.

core_game_loop.content:
#### Overview
My Little Scythe is a family-friendly competitive adventure game set in the Kingdom of Pomme. Each player controls 2 animal seekers racing to earn 4 trophies by completing quests, winning pie fights, delivering resources to Castle Everfree, upgrading abilities, collecting pies, and maintaining friendship.

#### Turn Structure
On your turn, you perform exactly **one** of three actions:
1. **Move** — Move your seekers across the hex map and optionally deliver resources.
2. **Seek** — Roll dice to generate new resources and place them on the map.
3. **Make** — Spend resources you've collected to upgrade, craft spells, or bake pies.

After resolving your action, check if you've earned any new trophies. If you now have 4 or more trophies, the game enters its final phase. Play continues until all players have had an equal number of turns, then the player with the most trophies wins (with friendship as the tiebreaker).

#### Resource Flow
The core economic loop works as follows:
- **Seek** places apples and gems onto the map from the supply.
- **Move** lets your seekers pick up resources from their hex and carry them toward Castle Everfree.
- **Delivering resources** to Castle Everfree earns progress toward the delivery trophies (apple delivery and gem delivery).
- **Make** converts resources in your seekers' possession into upgrades, spells, or pies.

#### Friendship & Combat
- The **Friendship Track** measures your standing in the kingdom. Friendly actions (delivering resources, certain quest choices) raise friendship. Aggressive actions (attacking other seekers, taking resources from occupied hexes) lower friendship.
- **Combat** occurs when your seeker moves into a hex occupied by an opponent's seeker. Both players secretly bid pie tokens behind their screens. The higher bidder wins; the loser's seeker retreats to their base camp. Combat wins earn a trophy, but attacking lowers your friendship.

#### Victory Condition
The first player to place their **4th trophy** triggers the endgame. Trophies can be earned in 8 different categories, but you only need 4. After equal turns, the player with the most trophies wins.

detailed_rules.content:
#### Action 1: Move
- Move **one or both** of your seekers up to the number of hexes shown on your Move action space (default 2, upgradeable to 3).
- Movement is split freely between your two seekers (e.g., move one seeker 2 hexes, or each seeker 1 hex).
- Your seekers **pick up all resources** (apples and gems) from any hex they move through or stop on. Resources ride along with the seeker.
- **Delivering to Castle Everfree**: When a seeker carrying resources enters the Castle Everfree center hex, you must deposit all carried resources into the castle. Each type (apples or gems) delivered counts toward separate delivery trophies. You earn the apple delivery trophy after delivering 3 apples total, and the gem delivery trophy after delivering 3 gems total.
- **Entering an occupied hex**: If your seeker enters a hex containing an opponent's seeker, you may initiate a **pie fight** (combat). You lose 1 friendship for attacking. If you pick up resources from a hex where an opponent is present, you also lose 1 friendship.

#### Action 2: Seek
- Roll the **4 story dice**. Each die shows either an apple, a gem, or a quest symbol.
- For each apple or gem result, take the corresponding token from the supply and place it on **any hex on the board** (your choice). You may spread them across different hexes or stack them.
- For each quest symbol rolled, draw 1 quest card and keep it face down. You may hold any number of quest cards.
- **Power-up bonus**: If your Seek action is powered up, you may place 1 additional resource of your choice (apple or gem) from the supply onto any hex after rolling.

#### Action 3: Make
Spend resources currently carried by your seekers to do one of the following:
- **Upgrade** (cost: 2 apples OR 2 gems) — Flip one of your 4 power-up tiles from its basic side to its upgraded side. Power-ups permanently enhance your Move or Make actions. Earning your first upgrade grants the upgrade trophy.
- **Craft a Magic Spell** (cost: 1 apple + 1 gem) — Draw 2 magic spell cards, keep 1, and return the other to the bottom of the deck. Spells are one-time-use abilities played on your turn (e.g., extra movement, resource theft, friendship boosts). Keep spells hidden behind your screen.
- **Bake Pies** (cost: 1 apple OR 1 gem per pie) — Convert resources into pie tokens stored behind your screen. Pies are used for combat bidding and some quest completions. Collecting 3 pies earns the pie trophy.

#### Pie Fights (Combat)
1. Combat triggers when your seeker enters a hex with an opponent's seeker and you choose to fight (or are forced to if both seekers share the hex at end of movement).
2. The **attacker loses 1 friendship** immediately.
3. Both players simultaneously reveal a secret bid of pie tokens from behind their screens (minimum 0).
4. **Highest bid wins**. Ties go to the defender.
5. All bid pies are **spent** (returned to the supply) by both players.
6. The **loser's seeker** is moved back to that player's base camp. Any resources the losing seeker carried are dropped on the battle hex.
7. The **winner** earns the combat trophy (if they don't already have it) and may pick up any resources on the hex.

#### Quests
- To attempt a quest, you must have a quest card and at least one seeker on a **quest hex** (marked with a scroll icon on the board).
- Each quest card presents **two options**: a generous choice that grants a smaller reward but increases friendship, and a greedy choice that grants a larger reward but decreases friendship.
- Quest rewards include pies, resources, movement, spells, or friendship increases.
- Completing a quest earns the quest trophy (if you don't already have it). Discard the quest card after resolving it.

#### Friendship Track
- All players start at **3** on the Friendship Track (range 0-8).
- **Friendship increases**: Delivering resources to Castle Everfree (+1 per delivery), choosing the generous option on quests, certain spell effects.
- **Friendship decreases**: Attacking another player (-1), picking up resources from an occupied hex (-1), choosing the greedy quest option.
- Reaching friendship level **6** earns the friendship trophy.
- Friendship serves as the **tiebreaker** for total trophies at game end.

#### Power-Up Tiles
- Each player has 4 power-up tiles (2 for Move, 2 for Make), starting on their basic side.
- **Move power-ups**: Increase total movement points from 2 to 3, or allow moving through hexes occupied by opponents without triggering combat.
- **Make power-ups**: Reduce upgrade costs by 1 resource, or gain an extra pie when baking.
- Flip a tile to its upgraded side when you perform the Upgrade action via Make.

#### Automountie (Solo Mode)
- The Automountie is an AI opponent controlled by a simple decision flowchart.
- On the Automountie's turn, draw an action token to determine Move, Seek, or Make.
- Movement follows a priority system: move toward Castle Everfree if carrying resources, otherwise move toward the nearest resources.
- The Automountie earns trophies using the same rules as human players.
- You win solo mode by reaching 4 trophies before the Automountie does.

scoring_and_endgame.content:
#### Trophy Categories
There are **8 possible trophies**, but you only need **4** to trigger the endgame. Each trophy can only be earned once per player.

1. **Quest Trophy** — Complete at least 1 quest card.
2. **Combat Trophy** — Win at least 1 pie fight.
3. **Apple Delivery Trophy** — Deliver a total of 3 apples to Castle Everfree.
4. **Gem Delivery Trophy** — Deliver a total of 3 gems to Castle Everfree.
5. **Upgrade Trophy** — Perform at least 1 upgrade (flip a power-up tile).
6. **Pie Trophy** — Accumulate 3 or more pie tokens at one time.
7. **Friendship Trophy** — Reach level 6 on the Friendship Track.
8. **Spell Trophy** — Craft and use at least 2 magic spells during the game.

#### Endgame Trigger
- When any player places their **4th trophy token** on the trophy track, the endgame is triggered.
- Continue play so that **all players have taken an equal number of turns** (finish the current round).
- Other players may still earn trophies during these remaining turns.

#### Final Scoring & Tiebreakers
1. The player with the **most trophies** wins.
2. If tied on trophies, the player with the **higher friendship level** wins.
3. If still tied, the player with **more pie tokens** remaining wins.
4. If still tied, the players share the victory.

#### Important Scoring Notes
- Trophies are **permanent** once earned. Losing pies in combat does not remove the pie trophy if you previously had 3.
- Friendship can decrease after earning the friendship trophy, but the trophy is not lost.
- There is no bonus for earning more than 4 trophies, but additional trophies help in tiebreaker situations.
- In solo mode, you must reach 4 trophies before the Automountie. If the Automountie reaches 4 first, you lose.

beginner_strategy.content:
#### Opening Priorities (First 3-4 Turns)
- **Seek early to seed the map**. Your first turn should almost always be Seek. Rolling dice and placing resources strategically near your base camp gives your seekers easy pickups on future turns.
- **Aim for 2 quick trophies**. The delivery trophies (apple and gem) are the most straightforward path. Seek to place resources near your seekers, then Move to pick them up and deliver to Castle Everfree.
- **Upgrade early**. Spending 2 matching resources on your first upgrade pays dividends all game. The extra movement point (2 to 3) is especially strong, letting you cross more of the map per turn.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Neglecting friendship**. New players often attack freely without realizing how costly friendship drops are. The friendship trophy at level 6 is one of the easiest trophies to earn — but only if you avoid unnecessary fights. Every attack costs you 1 friendship.
- **Hoarding resources on seekers**. Resources carried by seekers are vulnerable to pie fights. Deliver them to Castle Everfree promptly rather than stockpiling.
- **Ignoring quests**. Quest cards are free trophies if you can reach a quest hex. Always pick the generous option early in the game — the friendship boost is worth more than the extra resources from the greedy option.
- **Spending all pies in one fight**. Pie tokens are hard to replace. Never bid everything in a single combat. Keep at least 1 pie as a deterrent against future attacks.
- **Tunnel-visioning on one trophy type**. You need 4 different trophies. Spreading your efforts across delivery, upgrades, friendship, and quests is more efficient than chasing combat repeatedly.

#### Key Strategic Decisions
- **Generous vs. Greedy quest choices**: Generous options raise friendship and help secure the friendship trophy. Greedy options give better immediate rewards. Early game, friendship is usually more valuable; late game, the greedy option can clinch a final trophy.
- **When to fight**: Combat earns a trophy but costs friendship and pies. Ideal fights are when you're already at high friendship and your opponent is carrying resources you want. Avoid fights below friendship level 4.
- **Resource placement on Seek**: When you Seek, you choose where to place resources. Place them near your own seekers for easy pickup, or far from opponents to deny them resources. Placing on quest hexes can set up future quest attempts.
- **Upgrade order**: The extra movement power-up is almost always the best first upgrade. More movement means faster deliveries and better map control. The baking bonus (extra pie per bake) is strong second if you plan to pursue the combat or pie trophies.
